label: Squarish 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED — ensuite sandwiched between master and standard in the private column; common T&B at rear-middle, accessible from dining (public) and standard (private). LDK is separate living + dining + kitchen, stacked front-to-rear.

adjacencies.note: ensuite is master's exclusive — door opens north from master
ensuite north wall touches standard south — no door, acoustic+privacy buffer
shared plumbing wall between common bath and kitchen — wet stacks in one rear bay

common is publicly accessible — door on common south wall to dining. NOTE: standard↔common bath_door adjacency was removed (2026-06-05) to make this topology swap-friendly. Common bath is now reached only via dining (publicly), not from standard's bedroom. Trade-off: lose the bedroom-side private door; gain the ability to flip master/standard placement via brief swap_master_standard.

master door east into dining — placed on the L-extension (rect2) east wall, hinged at the HIGH (north) end via the bedroom stack-bias. This clusters master's door right next to standard's door at the bedroom-wing boundary.

standard door east into dining (rear of the LDK column, in front of kitchen). 1.1 m floor = 0.8 m clear panel + 2x 0.1 m frame stops + 0.1 m leeway. Half-step compromise between the bare-minimum 1.0 m (which puts the door right at the corner) and the 1.2 m used in clustered topologies — gives standard's door breathing room without making the 11x11 NCP lot infeasible.
living and dining flow as one open public space — no wall (ld style)
dining flows into kitchen — open pass-through, no wall (PH practice)

soft_proximities.note: pull common toward standard's east wall (the primary user) when there's slack

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_distributed_baths_ld
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Bath area is the HIGHEST priority soft constraint for this topology — both baths floor at 3.0 m² preferred-low in auto_apply (always applied) so the ensuite reads consistently at preferred across NCP and carport variants. Without this, the carport variants drop the tier and ensuite collapses to its 0.9 m corridor minimum, while NCP variants keep their preferred ensuite. To make room for the bath floors on tight carport lots, kitchen's auto-floor is at the rules.json hard min (1.5 m); the preferred 2.0 m galley sits in preferred_apply and may relax. Master is hard-capped at 18 m² area + 4.5 m greatest dim so its priority-4 weight doesn't steal middle-band depth from ensuite.

notes: v2 catalog topology #4 — 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED. The two baths are NOT
clustered — they sit in different bands of the building. The ensuite is
sandwiched between master (front) and standard (rear) IN the private
column, so the private wing reads as [master, ensuite, standard] front-
to-rear. The common T&B sits at the rear-MIDDLE, between standard (west)
and kitchen (east), serving as both the bedroom-side bath (standard's
interior door) and the public-side bath (door to dining for guest use).
LDK is the 'ld' style: separate living + dining + kitchen stacked front-
to-rear in the public column. Living hosts the entry. The LDK rule that
kitchen can sit side-by-side with dining only when living is in front of
both is satisfied here trivially because we explicitly stack the three.
Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_preference='right'):
Rear:  [standard         | T&B | kitchen                            ]
Mid:   [ensuite           | dining                                  ]
Front: [master            | living                                  ]
Private/public split: master + ensuite + standard on the LEFT, living +
dining + kitchen on the RIGHT. Common T&B is zone='service' — it is
geometrically on the public-side x range (east of standard) but not
constrained by the zone_split. Floor band: ~60-80 m² (similar to the
clustered_baths_gr band; the distributed bath block adds slightly less
circulation overhead than a hall variant but more than a single bath).
Carport: 3.0 m x 6.0 m at the front-right (Item 2 default). The 1 m x
4 m exceeding the side+front setbacks becomes a building_void carved
from the LDK column's front-right corner — same L-shape pattern as the
clustered_baths topologies. Brief.carport_preference='none' strips the
void; brief.carport_preference='left' mirrors the topology.
ACOUSTIC NOTE: when ensuite_alcove_joins_master triggers (bedroom column
wider than ~3.0 m), the L-extension's north edge butts directly against
standard's south edge — a short bedroom-to-bedroom wall without the
ensuite acting as an acoustic buffer there. PH practice mitigates by
placing master's closet (or standard's closet) against this segment.
This is a future fixture-placement concern, not a topology constraint.
